content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 25, 1989




Judith S. Davis
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson,
Parrinello & Mueller
770 L Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                        Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
                                             Our File No. I-89-460

Dear Ms. Davis:

     You have asked for confirmation of our telephone conversation
of August 3, 1989, concerning the date that the opinion of the
Court of Appeal in Taxpayers to Limit Campaign Spending v. Fair
Political Practices Commission (2d Civ. No. B039177) becomes
"final." On August 8, 1989, I informed you that we are unable to
provide written information of that conversation at this time,
pending resolution of the question in the form of a Commission
opinion.

     Attached is a copy of the opinion request and the draft
opinion, which will be considered at the Commission meeting to be
held September 6, 1989.

     If you have any questions concerning this letter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

KED/aa

Attachments




428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER

SAN FRANCISCO

650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800

A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

770 L STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752

August 4, 1989

Ms. Kathryn Donovan
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Finality of Court's Opinion in Taxpayers to Limit
Campaign Spending v. Fair Political Practices
Commission

Dear Kathy:

To confirm our telephone conversation yesterday, the
opinion of the Court of Appeals in the Taxpayers suit will not
become final until, at the earliest, August 31, 1989 -- 30 days
after it was filed. Until then, legislators and legislative
candidates may continue to accept political contributions,
despite the fact that 1989 is an "off-year" for all but those
involved in special elections.

You also mentioned that, based on your recent
conversations with the court, and assuming no appeal, the opinion
might not be final for all purposes until October 2, 1989, when
the time for the Supreme Court to order review on its own motion
expires. You were of the opinion that the provisions of
Proposition 68 found by the court to be valid and enforceable
would not be "deemed to have been in effect" until that date. I
would very much appreciate receiving written confirmation that
the October 2, 1989 date does indeed represent the "date on which
this opinion becomes final" referred to by the court as the
effective date of the provisions of Proposition 68 found to be
valid and enforceable.

Thank you so much for your time and courtesy.

Very truly yours,

Judith S. Davis

JSD:en

sections.question_synthetic: The requester seeks written confirmation of when a Court of Appeal opinion in Taxpayers to Limit Campaign Spending v. FPPC becomes 'final' for purposes of determining the effective date of Proposition 68 provisions, specifically whether the operative date is August 31, 1989 (30 days after filing) or October 2, 1989 (when Supreme Court review period expires).
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC General Counsel declined to provide written confirmation of the conversation at that time, indicating the matter would be resolved through a Commission opinion to be considered at the September 6, 1989 Commission meeting.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of two letters: incoming request from attorney Judith Davis (August 4) and FPPC response from General Counsel Kathryn Donovan (August 25). No formal Q/A or conclusion sections present; content extracted from letter body. Confidence reduced due to lack of structured sections and pendency of final opinion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester seeks written confirmation of when a Court of Appeal opinion in Taxpayers to Limit Campaign Spending v. FPPC becomes 'final' for purposes of determining the effective date of Proposition 68 provisions, specifically whether the operative date is August 31, 1989 (30 days after filing) or October 2, 1989 (when Supreme Court review period expires).

CONCLUSION: The FPPC General Counsel declined to provide written confirmation of the conversation at that time, indicating the matter would be resolved through a Commission opinion to be considered at the September 6, 1989 Commission meeting.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a request for written confirmation regarding the finality date of a Court of Appeal opinion affecting Proposition 68's effective date. The FPPC declines immediate written confirmation, stating the question will be addressed through a formal Commission opinion.
